signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + Retail
signals.text: AUD/USD COT LF net long +41,675 (bullish spec positioning) vs retail 89% short (extreme retail short). HV30 at 8.2%, ATM IV at 9.4% — options pricing a 1.2 vol-pt premium over realized. 25d RR at −0.14: mild put skew despite the bullish price trend — market hedging rather than chasing. Late-trend caution signal. A LF delta turning positive or retail capitulation would invalidate the caution read.

signals.title: GBP/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: BoE 4.50% vs Fed 3.75% — 75bp GBP carry premium supports the pair structurally. GBP LF net long +28k, AM constructive — positioning aligned with the carry read. GBP/USD at 1.347; 1.3500 is the next resistance on a continuation. Watch for a break above 1.3500 to confirm the bullish trend.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT + OIS
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short −17,090 (specs short) vs RBNZ OIS hike 100% priced. Specs are fading a hawkish CB. If RBNZ delivers or signals hike, short squeeze risk is elevated. NZD/USD at 0.5859; watch for a move above 0.5900 to confirm the bullish trend.

signals.title: GBP/JPY — CB + Carry
signals.text: BoE 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75% — 300bp GBP carry premium supports GBP structurally. GBP/JPY at 215.061; 216.00 is the next resistance on a continuation. Carry advantage favors GBP, but risk-off JPY bid competes.
